rest_code_table.description: Code Tables in Alma contains list of codes and their related description. When using Alma RESTful API, codes should be sent to the system. Descriptions are usually returned in the GET action as a desc attribute of the relevant field. Code Tables are usually defined in the scope of the institution. There are few Code Tables that might be defined in the scope of a library - e.g. Item Policy. Descriptions in the Code Table might be in any of the institution's languages. Examples of code tables: PhysicalMaterialTypeJobTitles , UserGroups , UserBlockTypes. See [Working with the code tables API](https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/blog/Working-with-the-code-tables-API) for more details.

sub_system.description: The code table's related sub system. Possible codes are listed in 'Sub Systems' [code table](https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/blog/Working-with-the-code-tables-API).

patron_facing.description: Indication whether the code table is patron facing or not.

updated_by.description: The last staff user which updated the code table. Output parameter.

update_date.description: The last date in which the code table was updated. Output parameter.

language.description: The language in which the code table is retrieved. Default is the institution's default language (default_language in the Customer Parameters code table).

scope.description: Code Tables are usually defined in the scope of the institution. There are few Code Tables that might be defined in the scope of a library - e.g. Item Policy.

institution_id.description: The institution in which the code table is defined.

library_id.description: The library in which the code table is defined. Relevant only for Code Tables that might be defined in the scope of a library - e.g. Item Policy.

default.description: Indication if this is the default row. Optional. Default is False.

enabled.description: Indication if this is an enabled row. Optional. Default is True.

updated_by.description: The last staff user which updated the row. Output parameter.

update_date.description: The last date in which the row was updated. Output parameter.
